stage: 15.E.3 follow-up — software vs production across all Qwen3-4B-AWQ shapes
summary: 9/12 cases at M in {4, 8}: software wins by 1.10-1.21x.

2/12 cases at M=1 on small-K layers: production WINS HARD: q_o by 2.13x, down by 2.98x. The awq_gemv kernel's GEMV-optimised access pattern beats the naive software dot-product when K is small (cache fits, kt_mloop's weight-reuse is in its sweet spot).
Stage 15.E.3 negative-result hypothesis 1 (microbench was a single-shape window into a multi-shape model) is CONFIRMED.
implications_for_threshold: Original M <= 8 gate is wrong for c=1 decode (M=1). The two M=1 losses (q_o, down) per layer × 28 layers = enough negative drag to wipe out the wins on gate/up_proj at the same M=1.

Refined gate (informed by data): 4 <= M <= 8 enables hybrid; M=1 stays on production. This routes c=1 decode entirely through awq_gemv (current behaviour), and only c=4 / c=8 decode see the hybrid path.

But the e2e bench at c=4 / c=8 already showed ~0 % lift even though software wins per-call by 10-20 %. Hypothesis 2 from 15.E.3 (per-call launch overhead amortisation differs in microbench vs real workload) is the remaining unknown — ruling it out would need either (a) profiling tile_mma launches under nvprof, or (b) reducing per-call overhead in the dispatcher itself.

Net: even with the refined threshold the projected e2e win is at best ~5 % at c=4 (Amdahl: 0.91 slice × 1.15 layer-win = 1.14, but the c=4 slice may be smaller per Stage 14-C, giving 1.05-1.08 e2e). Not the 1.20x originally projected. Stage 15.D-body (tensor cores) remains the path that actually delivers.
decision: Update ADR 0016 with this shape-aware data. Mark the M <= 8 threshold as PROVISIONALLY 4 <= M <= 8 (excluding M=1) for any future hybrid-on production deployment.
Keep VLLM_AWQ_HYBRID OFF by default (15.E.3). Real users may toggle it on once the threshold is correct; gain remains ~0-5 %.
Stage 15.D-body is still the priority: tensor cores beat both software AND production across the full M sweep.
